theme: Merge clusters permanently toward a good partition
title: Chain-Trap Dendrogram: Merging Toward a Kept Cut
strategies: trivial: reproduce the checker's own naive fixed index-pairing merge order (ignores geometry and affinity entirely)
greedy: classic single-linkage -- always merge the two currently-closest clusters by centroid distance, oblivious to the bonus/penalty matrix and to the horizon-weighted schedule

strong: choose the K-cluster target partition first via a correlation-clustering-style greedy on the bonus matrix (judging every merge by the final cut it commits toward), build each target cluster's internal merge tree by local nearest-centroid, then globally schedule all merges by descending raw cost so expensive-but-necessary merges land early (low horizon weight) and cheap merges land late (high horizon weight)
invalid: emits a structurally-shaped but infeasible merge sequence (references a cluster id that was never created) to probe the feasibility gate
